codeReview.summary: Clean, minimal DOM reorder in src/server.ts that moves From Token and To Token blocks before the Direction Toggle and Amount. The diff touches only 2 files (server.ts and server.integration.test.ts) with 70 lines changed. Tab order follows DOM order naturally (no explicit tabindex attributes needed). URL param restoration uses getElementById which is DOM-order-independent, so it's unaffected by the reorder. Tests were properly updated to assert the new order: chain < wallet < from < to < direction < amount < submit < slippage. No CSS changes were required since form groups are full-width block elements. The commit message is descriptive and accurate.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature was actually implemented in a prior worker session (a14b1882-a533-44db-9fb7-207b6719113e) and the assigned worker session (0e11f845-e4d7-46b1-8f1e-ea0c354c7218) only verified the existing work. The handoff correctly reports this, but the feature's workerSessionIds array shows both sessions. No action needed, but this pattern of a second session re-verifying an already-committed feature means the orchestrator could potentially skip re-assignment if the commit already exists.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff says 'The ui-form-reorder feature was already implemented in commit 389a521'. Feature workerSessionIds: ['a14b1882-a533-44db-9fb7-207b6719113e', '0e11f845-e4d7-46b1-8f1e-ea0c354c7218'].

summary: The ui-form-reorder feature is correctly implemented. The DOM order in INDEX_HTML was changed from Chain → Wallet → Amount → Direction → From → To → Submit to Chain → Wallet → From → To → Direction → Amount → Submit. The change is minimal and focused (34 lines in server.ts, 36 lines in tests). Tab order matches visual order by default since no tabindex attributes are used. URL param restore is DOM-order-independent. Tests verify the new order. No issues found.
